trigger_condition: A visible undergranulated leaf groups topics too broadly, but the missing split is easy to see.
why_this_crosses_the_line: It lowers study precision and creates extra work, even if it does not yet poison trust globally.

trigger_condition: Repeated visible placement errors or repeated boundary inconsistency appear across neighboring branches.
why_this_crosses_the_line: At this point the problem is no longer a one-off nuisance; it begins to question structural discipline.

trigger_condition: There is any credible sign that placement logic or current tree shape is influencing excerpt boundaries or grouping.
why_this_crosses_the_line: This is the point where the wrong stage may have contaminated excerpt truth, so normal study should pause until audited.

trigger_condition: The system repeatedly shows that similar passages receive materially different excerpt outputs depending on guidance mode or tree state.
why_this_crosses_the_line: Even if not every unit is proven wrong, the excerpting stage no longer feels firmly source-anchored.

trigger_condition: Changing the tree granulation changes rightful excerpt output in a systemic way.
why_this_crosses_the_line: That means taxonomy dependence has entered excerpt truth itself.

trigger_condition: A silent excerpt corruption is discovered after study has already been built on it.
why_this_crosses_the_line: This puts the source book, excerpting engine, and broader knowledge base in question rather than just one leaf.
